message:
fix(search,status): pass db_available flag to lexical asset inspection and harden semantic error preservation

The lexical fingerprint comparison previously used db_path.exists() to
decide whether to compute the current storage fingerprint. When the
database file exists but cannot be opened (e.g., path is a directory, or
WAL corruption), the fingerprint computation would fail with an
unhelpful error that masked the actual database open failure.

- Thread a db_available parameter from state_meta_json through
  inspect_search_assets and inspect_lexical_assets so the fingerprint
  computation is skipped when the database is known to be unopenable,
  preserving the root-cause diagnostic
- Fix Health CLI stale_threshold arg attribute: change `default` (string
  literal default) to `default_value_t` (typed default) for correct
  clap parsing
- Add test: inspect_search_assets_preserves_semantic_database_unavailable_signal
  verifies that when db_available=false, lexical status is not "error"
  and semantic reports "database_unavailable" with the correct fallback
- Add assertions in status_json and health_json robot tests ensuring
  semantic availability is not collapsed to a generic "load_failed"
  when the real cause is db unavailability

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
